hero_subtitle: SFTY connects risk signals, case management, and evidence to streamline suspicious activity reporting and support audit-ready FIU workflows within a secure supervisory layer.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: Reporting pipelines slow when data stays disconnected
problem_description: Regulators face operational delays and fragmented workflows when risk signals and case evidence remain isolated from formal reporting channels. This disconnection complicates audit readiness and hinders timely submission of defensible suspicious activity reports.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY connects fragmented risk signals, case evidence and supervisory reviews into one structured reporting workflow that supports audit-ready suspicious activity reporting and timely FIU regulatory outputs.
workflow_title: From suspicious activity to reporting package
workflow_description: SFTY structures risk signals into cases, gathers evidence and prepares comprehensive reports to support financial intelligence unit workflows and regulatory submissions.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. One supervisory layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented risk signals, cases, evidence and reporting into a single secure supervisory environment for structured FIU workflows and audit-ready outputs.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY delivers a secure, auditable platform tailored for government reporting pipelines where access governance, evidence-grade logging and procurement standards are critical.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how reporting pipelines support FIU workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how suspicious activity links to case management
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logs support regulatory reports
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe multi-provider data orchestration in reporting pipelines
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for financial intelligence units?
faq_1_answer: SFTY helps FIUs connect risk signals, cases, evidence and reports in a single supervised pipeline for digital asset oversight and reporting workflows.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY structure suspicious activity reporting?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures workflows from alerts to case creation, evidence collection, decision logs and SAR/STR-style reports aligned with FIU investigation needs.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready supervisory evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, supervisory logs and evidence packages to support review readiness and defensible outputs in regulated environments.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing FIU data systems?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY supports modular integration with government registries, blockchain data, analytics providers and internal FIU systems via secure and governed interfaces.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing analytics or reporting tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates above existing tools and data sources, connecting outputs into supervised workflows rather than replacing analytics or reporting platforms.
final_title: Review supervisory reporting workflows with SFTY
final_description: See how SFTY connects risk signals, cases and evidence into audit-ready FIU reporting pipelines and supervisory workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
